relevance_summary: CISD2 is implicated in the pathophysiology of Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH) through its role in autophagy regulation and immune response.
citations: Bioinformatics and experimental validation of ferroptosis-related genes in steroid-induced osteonecrosis of the femoral head. (2025). https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40421419/

relevance_summary: TSTA3 is a promising biomarker for the early detection of Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH), linked to immune cell infiltration and differential expression in affected patients.
citations: ARG2, MAP4K5 and TSTA3 as Diagnostic Markers of Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head and Their Correlation With Immune Infiltration. (2021). https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34381494/

relevance_summary: BPGM is identified as a potential biomarker for early detection of Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH) due to its involvement in erythrocyte regulation and immune processes.

relevance_summary: HEMGN is identified as a potential diagnostic biomarker for Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH) due to its downregulation in affected patients.
